 {"id":"gt-3pp","title":"Support numeric shortcuts in mail read (e.g., 'mail read 1')","description":"When inbox shows numbered messages like:
* 1. gm-19b29031...  2025-12-16  mayor  Subject...
* 2. gm-19b26d51...  2025-12-16         Subject...

Users should be able to run 'gt mail read 1' instead of needing the full message ID 'gt mail read gm-19b29031f6a172206'.

- Map numeric indices to actual message IDs
- Accept both numeric shortcuts and full IDs in 'mail read' command","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:15:07.857939-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T13:15:29.273066-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-3tz","title":"CLI: polecat commands (add, list, wake, sleep, decommission)","description":"GGT is missing most polecat management commands that PGT has.

Missing Commands:
- gt polecat add <rig> <name> - Add polecat to rig (creates clone)
- gt polecat list [<rig>] - List polecats with state
- gt polecat info <polecat> - Show detailed info
- gt polecat wake <polecat> - Mark available
- gt polecat sleep <polecat> - Mark unavailable  
- gt polecat decommission <polecat> - Remove polecat safely

PGT Reference: gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/polecat_cmd.py

Notes:
- spawn exists but doesn't cover management
- wake/sleep are in polecat manager but not CLI
- decommission should check for uncommitted work","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:31.326692-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:03:14.462338-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:03:14.462338-08:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of gt-u1j.17 which has more detail"}
 {"id":"gt-3x1","title":"Update Refinery to use Beads merge queue","description":"Replace branch discovery with Beads queue in the Refinery module:

Current (internal/refinery/manager.go):
- Scans for polecat/* branches
- Creates MR objects on-the-fly

New:
- Pull from Beads: bd ready --type=merge-request
- Process each MR
- Close with merge commit: bd close <id> --reason="Merged at <sha>"
- Handle failures: bd update <id> --status=blocked --reason="..."

The Engineer (agent) becomes Beads-native.
The Refinery (module) provides the infrastructure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:37.96436-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:32.911782-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x1","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:55.812433-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x1","depends_on_id":"gt-svi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:03:12.814463-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"gt-3x1.1","title":"Engineer main loop: poll for ready merge-requests","description":"Implement the Engineer's main processing loop.

Loop structure:
1. Query: bd ready --type=merge-request
2. If empty: sleep(poll_interval), continue
3. Select highest priority, oldest MR
4. Claim: bd update <id> --status=in_progress
5. Process (delegate to other subtasks)
6. Repeat

Configuration:
- poll_interval: from rig config (default 30s)
- max_concurrent: from rig config (default 1)

The loop should be interruptible and handle graceful shutdown.

+{"id":"gt-3x1.1","title":"Engineer main loop: poll for ready merge-requests","description":"Implement the Engineer's main processing loop.

Loop structure:
1. Query: bd ready --type=merge-request
2. If empty: sleep(poll_interval), continue
3. Select highest priority, oldest MR
4. Claim: bd update <id> --status=in_progress
5. Process (delegate to other subtasks)
6. Repeat

Configuration:

- max_concurrent: from rig config (default 1)

The loop should be interruptible and handle graceful shutdown.

 {"id":"gt-3x1.2","title":"Fetch and conflict check: git operations for MR","description":"Implement git operations for MR processing.

Steps:
1. git fetch origin <mr.branch>
2. git checkout <mr.target> (main or integration/xxx)
3. git merge --no-commit --no-ff <mr.branch> (test merge)
4. Check for conflicts
5. If conflicts: abort and return Failure(conflict, files)
6. If clean: abort (actual merge in next step)

Helper functions:
- FetchBranch(branch string) error
- CheckConflicts(source, target string) ([]string, error)

Reference: docs/merge-queue-design.md#process-merge-steps","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:50:58.99193-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:17:47.781432-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:17:47.781432-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in commit 999abe6","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x1.2","depends_on_id":"gt-3x1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:50:58.993973-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x1.2","depends_on_id":"gt-3x1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:53:10.066159-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-3x1.3","title":"Merge execution: merge, test, push","description":"Implement the actual merge execution.

Steps:
1. git checkout <mr.target>
2. git merge <mr.branch> --no-ff -m 'Merge <branch>: <title>'
3. If config.run_tests:
   - Run test_command (from config)
   - If failed: git reset --hard HEAD~1, return Failure(tests_failed)
4. git push origin <mr.target>
5. Return Success(merge_commit=HEAD)

Configuration:
- run_tests: bool (default true)
- test_command: string (default 'go test ./...')

Handle push failures with retry logic.

Reference: docs/merge-queue-design.md#process-merge-steps","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:00.742994-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:00.742994-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x1.3","depends_on_id":"gt-3x1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:00.744975-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x1.3","depends_on_id":"gt-3x1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:53:10.163097-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-3x1.4","title":"Failure handling: assign back to worker, add labels","description":"Handle merge failures appropriately.

Failure types and actions:
| Failure     | Action                                      |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------|
| conflict    | Add needs-rebase label, assign to worker    |
| tests_fail  | Add needs-fix label, assign to worker       |
| build_fail  | Add needs-fix label, assign to worker       |
| flaky_test  | Retry once, then treat as tests_fail        |
| push_fail   | Retry with backoff, escalate if persistent  |

Actions:
1. bd update <id> --status=open --assignee=<worker>
2. bd update <id> --labels=<failure-label>
3. Send mail to worker explaining failure
4. Log failure details

Reference: docs/merge-queue-design.md#handling-failures","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:17.238066-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:17.238066-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x1.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:17.240001-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x1.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:53:10.281038-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"gt-4my","title":"Doctor check: Worker health and stuck detection","description":"Detect and report stuck workers via gt doctor.

## Checks

### WorkerHealthCheck
- List all active workers (polecats with state=working)
- Check last activity timestamp for each
- Flag as potentially stuck if no progress for configurable threshold (default: 30 min)
- Check if Witness is running for the rig
- Verify Witness last heartbeat time

### Stuck Detection Criteria
- Polecat state=working but session not running
- Polecat state=working but output unchanged for threshold
- Witness not responding to health checks
- Multiple polecats in same rig all stuck

## Output

```
[WARN] Workers in rig 'wyvern' may be stuck:
  - Toast: working for 45m, no recent output
  - Capable: working for 52m, session not found
  - Witness: last heartbeat 20m ago
  
  Suggestions:
  - gt witness status wyvern
  - gt capture wyvern/Toast 50
  - gt stop --rig wyvern (kill all)
```

## Auto-Fix

Cannot auto-fix stuck workers (risk of data loss), but can:
- Restart Witness daemon if crashed
- Send warning mail to Mayor","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:59.265062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:24:34.882466-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4my","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.565606-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-4my","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:42.068149-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4nn","title":"Molecules: Composable Workflow Beads","description":"## Summary

Molecules are crystallized workflow patterns stored as Beads issues.
When instantiated, the molecule creates child beads forming a DAG.

## Key Insight: Molecules ARE Beads

Per HOP Decision 001: Beads IS the ledger. Molecules don't get a separate YAML format - they're issues with `type: molecule` containing prose-based step definitions.

Agents don't need rigid schemas. They parse natural language natively. A molecule is just instructions with enough structure for tooling.

## Example: Engineer in a Box

```markdown
id: mol-xyz
type: molecule
title: Engineer in a Box

This workflow takes a task from design to merge.

## Step: design
Think carefully about architecture. Consider existing patterns, 
trade-offs, testability.

## Step: implement
Write clean code. Follow codebase conventions.
Needs: design

## Step: review  
Review for bugs, edge cases, style issues.
Needs: implement

## Step: test
Write and run tests. Cover happy path and edge cases.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge via refinery.
Needs: review, test
```

## Instantiation

```bash
# Attach molecule when spawning
gt spawn --issue gt-abc --molecule mol-xyz

# Creates child beads atomically:
gt-abc.design     ← ready first
gt-abc.implement  ← blocked by design  
gt-abc.review     ← blocked by implement
gt-abc.test       ← blocked by implement
gt-abc.submit     ← blocked by review, test
```

Each step issue gets an `instantiated-from` edge to the molecule (with step metadata).

## Why This Matters

1. **Unified data plane**: Everything in Beads, no parallel YAML channel
2. **AI-native**: Prose instructions, not rigid schemas
3. **Error isolation**: Each step is a checkpoint - failure doesn't lose progress
4. **Scales with AI**: As agents get smarter, they handle more complex molecules

## Implementation Primitives

- `ParseMoleculeSteps()`: Extract steps from prose (convention-based)
- `InstantiateMolecule()`: Atomic transaction creating all steps + edges  
- `instantiated-from` edge type: Track provenance
- Parameterization: `{{variable}}` substitution from context map","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:24.573068-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:52.411317-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-4nn.1","title":"Molecule schema: YAML format for workflow definitions","description":"Define the YAML schema for molecule definitions:

```yaml
molecule: <name>
version: 1
description: "Human description"
steps:
  - id: <step-id>
    title: "Step title"
    prompt: "Instructions for agent"
    depends: [<other-step-ids>]  # optional
    tier: haiku|sonnet|opus      # optional, default from config
    timeout: 30m                 # optional
```

Store molecules in:
- `<rig>/molecules/<name>.yaml` for rig-specific
- `<town>/molecules/<name>.yaml` for town-wide

Built-in molecules to ship:
- engineer-in-box: design→code→review→test→submit
- quick-fix: implement→test→submit
- research: investigate→document","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:49.441267-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:32.629327-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:32.629327-08:00","close_reason":"Pivoting away from YAML schema. Molecules should be Beads issues with prose-based step definitions, not a separate YAML format. This keeps the data plane unified in Beads (per HOP Decision 001) and leverages AI flexibility - agents can interpret prose instructions without rigid schemas.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.1","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:49.442723-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-4nn.2","title":"Molecule instantiation: create child beads from template","description":"When instantiating a molecule on a work bead:

## Transaction Flow

1. Parse molecule's `## Step:` sections from description
2. Begin SQLite transaction
3. For each step, create child issue:
   - ID: `{parent-id}.{step-ref}` or generated
   - Title: step title (from header or first line)
   - Description: step prose instructions
   - Type: task
   - Priority: inherit from parent
4. Add `instantiated-from` edge from each step to molecule:
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata)
   VALUES (step_id, mol_id, 'instantiated-from', '{"step": "implement"}');
   ```
5. Wire inter-step dependencies from `Needs:` lines
6. Commit transaction (atomic - all or nothing)

## Parsing Conventions

```markdown
## Step: <ref>
<prose instructions>
Needs: <step>, <step>  # optional
Tier: haiku|sonnet|opus  # optional hint
```

## Parameterization

Steps can have `{{variable}}` placeholders:
```markdown
## Step: implement
Implement {{feature_name}} in {{target_file}}.
```

Context map provided at instantiation time.

## API

```go
func (s *Store) InstantiateMolecule(mol *Issue, parent *Issue, ctx map[string]string) ([]*Issue, error)
func ParseMoleculeSteps(description string) ([]MoleculeStep, error)
```

Implementation lives in `internal/beads/molecule.go`.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.071066-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:15:15.502672-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.072554-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-4nn.3","title":"Molecule CLI: bd molecule commands","description":"Add molecule commands to bd:

## Commands

```bash
bd molecule list              # List molecules (type: molecule)
bd molecule show <id>         # Show molecule with parsed steps
bd molecule parse <id>        # Validate and show parsed structure  
bd molecule instantiate <mol-id> --parent=<issue-id>  # Create steps
bd molecule instances <mol-id>  # Show all instantiations
```

## gt spawn integration

```bash
gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <mol-id>
```

This should:
1. Call `bd molecule instantiate` (creates child beads atomically)
2. Spawn polecat on first ready step
3. Polecat grinds through via `bd ready`

## Output Examples

```
$ bd molecule show mol-abc

mol-abc: Engineer in a Box
Type: molecule

Steps (5):
  design      → (ready first)
  implement   → Needs: design
  review      → Needs: implement
  test        → Needs: implement  
  submit      → Needs: review, test
  
Instances: 3
```

```
$ bd molecule instances mol-abc

Parent          Status    Created
gt-xyz          done      2025-12-15
gt-abc          active    2025-12-17 (3/5 complete)
gt-def          pending   2025-12-18
```","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:53.919884-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:15:38.500265-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:53.921621-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:03.048941-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4nn.4","title":"Built-in molecules: engineer-in-box, quick-fix, research","description":"Create built-in molecules as Beads issues:

## engineer-in-box

```markdown
id: mol-engineer-in-box
type: molecule
title: Engineer in a Box

Full workflow from design to merge.

## Step: design
Think carefully about architecture. Consider:
- Existing patterns in the codebase
- Trade-offs between approaches  
- Testability and maintainability

Write a brief design summary before proceeding.

## Step: implement
Write the code. Follow codebase conventions.
Needs: design

## Step: review
Self-review the changes. Look for:
- Bugs and edge cases
- Style issues
- Missing error handling
Needs: implement

## Step: test
Write and run tests. Cover happy path and edge cases.
Fix any failures before proceeding.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge via refinery.
Needs: review, test
```

## quick-fix

```markdown
id: mol-quick-fix
type: molecule  
title: Quick Fix

Fast path for small changes.

## Step: implement
Make the fix. Keep it focused.

## Step: test
Run relevant tests. Fix any regressions.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge.
Needs: test
```

## research

```markdown
id: mol-research
type: molecule
title: Research

Investigation workflow.

## Step: investigate
Explore the question. Search code, read docs, 
understand context. Take notes.

## Step: document
Write up findings. Include:
- What you learned
- Recommendations
- Open questions
Needs: investigate
```

## Storage

Built-in molecules live in `<town>/.beads/` as regular issues.
Created during `gt install` or `bd init`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.574565-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:15:38.8612-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.576587-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-51x","title":"Fix golangci-lint errcheck warnings (~160 issues)","description":"Running golangci-lint shows ~160 errcheck warnings for unchecked error returns.

Common patterns:
- t.SetEnvironment() return values
- os.WriteFile(), os.RemoveAll() 
- MarkFlagRequired() on cobra commands
- Various manager methods

Run: golangci-lint run ./...

Could batch fix with:
1. Add explicit _ = for intentionally ignored errors
2. Handle errors properly where they matter
3. Consider adding //nolint:errcheck for cobra flag setup","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:02:39.807659-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T15:02:39.807659-08:00"}
